title: Declaration adoption vs signing
prompt: State the calendar date on which most delegates signed the engrossed parchment of the Declaration of Independence, and say whether a broad claim that the delegates signed it on July 4, 1776 is supportable.
referenceSteps: Identify July 4, 1776 as the adoption/approval date of the Declaration text.
Distinguish adoption of the text from signing of the engrossed parchment.
Identify August 2, 1776 as the date on which most delegates signed the engrossed parchment.
Conclude that a broad claim that the delegates signed it on July 4, 1776 is not supportable in the common historical signing sense.
criticalSteps: Identify August 2, 1776 as the signing date for most delegates.
notes: Wave-1 dependency-chain case. Failure shape: plan answers the signing question by retrieving only adoption-focused material and silently treating July 4 as sufficient.

title: Magna Carta definitive version
prompt: Which issue of Magna Carta is the definitive version in English law? Name the issuing monarch and year.
referenceSteps: Retrieve a source that distinguishes the 1215 issue of Magna Carta from later reissues.
Identify the 1215 issue as the famous original issue under King John, but not the definitive operative English-law version for this framing.
Identify the 1225 reissue as the definitive version in this English-law framing.
Identify Henry III as the issuing monarch of the 1225 reissue.
criticalSteps: Identify 1225 as the legally definitive target for this framing.
notes: Wave-1 retrieval-sensitive case. Failure shape: plan retrieves generic 1215 material and treats that retrieval as sufficient.
